reviews.review_text:
Discussing,without any doubt the worst caravan park we have ever been in and we have been to a lot
Dumpster did not  work , toilets don’t flush properly, dead rats on awnings slab , 1/2 full of derelict permanents .
Owner who just wants to fine you for everything he can think of
Don’t go there
Just wow best people ever thankyou so much for a beautiful peaceful stay

Hadn't booked but no problem getting a powered site. Friendly and helpful reception staff. Site a bit cramped but adequate for us. Looks like there are long stay residents as well as travellers. Camp kitchen was useful and showers etc hot and clean. Laundry good and great to have washing lines!  A bit noisy at night due to other residents but otherwise a peaceful spot.

Stayed in a chalet with a friend and my dog.  Very friendly staff on arrival and more than willing to help but that’s about as good as it gets. The chalet is very dated.  First thing I noticed was the tiled flooring was very cracked and broken and didn’t look as though it had even been swept as it was covered in dog hair from a previous guest.  The walls have holes and reminded me of an 80’s mining camp donga with the marks on the walls.
Things don’t get much better at all.
The cutting board in the kitchen was broken in 2 pieces and we couldn’t light 2 burners on the gas stove.  I could have written a book on what has to be done to bring the place up to a reasonable standard but I will spare you the details.  The outside table wasn’t even wiped down🙁
Was the place worth $170 per night? Definitely not

Stayed a few times, camp spots are good.
Clean showers and toilets.
Staff are super friendly
Good cooking and cleaning facilities.
Rooms are basic but clean and reasonably priced.
Again the staff are super friendly and helpful
Had an excellent stay yet again. Clean, tidy and quiet. I'll be back.
I loved staying at this place. Peaceable environment.
Nice little budget option to stay in for a few nights while in Geraldton, good for families. Only criticism is rooms are a bit small but to be expected considering price.
Very nice quiet park to stay at.Check in    on arrival with genuine helpful kindness from owners and staff.Has a nice Pool and amenities to compliment.
Friendly staff good facilities and a great atmosphere will definitely stay again and again 😊 and my puppy is always well received here.

Had a cabin. Staff was absolutely amazing after I had to rebook. Lucky I was up for work tho and had to be up early anyway because the neighbours dog barking sometimes from 430am onwards was not that great to wake up too. Apart from that can't really fault it as I was gone early and came home late every day

We used the overflow at the beginning of the school holidays when everything was booked. The staff were really helpful and friendly and the overflow was a pleasant grassed area with heaps of room.
Also clean facilities and hot showers :)
A very friendly out of town campsite. The owners are lovely and very accommodating. We stayed her one night on the way up north for our holiday.
Lovely shady spots.  Good amenities.  Nice people.

Nice quiet friendly park with helpful staff. A big thank you to Steve who spent a good part of his day  repairing a water leak under our sink. Refused any payment. Very helpful in giving information especially about the wreath flowers and wildflowers.

Booked by phone from Billabong RH late in the afternoon without any problems. Site was great 👍😃
I will be visiting again
A great park ideally suited for all visited to Geraldton and surrounding areas, friendly staff and clean, we enjoyed. Thank You

The staff here are absolutely wonderful, friendly and welcoming!! They do their best to help everyone with any issues and provide a positive overall experience.
Thank you to Dean, Lisa, Steve and Jean for making the park a better place. Love you guys! xx
Chant
I camped, so room not applicable, booking via website was easy and the automated check-in process also easy. It’s a bit out of town, but on the right side for ongoing travel towards Carnarvon

Nice park, lots of permanents, but good for an overnight stay. The showers could do with a good shot of oil of cloves to kill the mould, then a bleaching to bring them up to speed. They are cleaned, just don't look it.
Good clean tidy place, suited us as we travelled through on our way ro Broome.

We stayed on a drive through site overnight, on our way North.  The location was great as it was away from the main roads, so very little traffic noise.  The staff were very friendly and helpful and the facilities were clean.

The park maintained a hold on my $200 security deposit for over a week after we stayed with them, which meant I couldn't access this money for most of my holiday.

I can see why they wanted a security deposit based upon how past guests had clearly treated the property. We paid $125 for a chalet cabin with a hole in a wall, cracked kitchen tiles that moved when you walked on them, and a plastic shower unit that felt like it was going to sink through the floor when you stood in it.

One star because the park was quiet, in a good location and the bed was comfortable.
Friendly staff. Clean toilets and a great area to stay 🥰
Neat and tidy. Made very welcome
Old school
Value for money.  Friendly helpful staff.  Very clearn toilets and showers.  Highly recommend and would happily stay again.
Good tranquil setting

Very pleasant stay. Good amenities, sunny, excellent staff.
Book online as it's cheaper. Unpowered site $34. Washing machine $6.
Good large unpowered sites. Amenities clean. Basic camp kitchen with picnic tables. A little bit out of town but an IGA close. $6 wash and dryers.
It is tired but friendly staff. Could do with some gravel in the pot holes on roads around the camp.

This park is very expensive for what you get,  $50 per night powered site, $6 for cold water only washing machines, no proper roads and very badly kept park. We've been paying a lot less for sites and laundry facilities with hot water the rest of this trip. The lady in reception was very pleasant unlike the rest of the caravan park,  we won't be back nor recommend it to anyone 😞

I stayed at an unpowered site. It was amazing!
Clean amenities and lovely staff.
Really quiet, safety and nice place.
Best caravan park I’ve ever been!
I’ll definitely stay here again.

Great location 10km from town centre felt very safe there. Lovely clean amenities and the staff are so friendly and helpful. Dog friendly and at the back of the park is an area you can exercise your dog.
Nice location out of town, toilet and shower block needs a refresh. Camp kitchen basic.
Very clean and friendly people easy to find it's out of town and 5 minutes to the beach ⛱️ 😎 enjoyed our and would recommend to everyone
Best site.
Stayed 5 nights. Nice and quiet as a bit outside of town. Staff very friendly and helpful . Facilities a bit old but clean and working. New pool.
Cheapest in geraldton, friendly welcoming atmosphere. Safe and secure.

Been travelling around wa for passed 6 mths thankyou to staff management and owners for beautiful stay not only the grounds but the kindness and hospitality me my partner and our Jimmy received thankyou for a beautiful stay. I recommend anyone travelling through Geraldton to stay here.
No way this place is getting any more of my money. Thanks a lot.
No problem at all. They had all we needed, clean amenities, power, water. Much cheaper that other parks around. Would book again.

I arrived after driving 757km. Just wanted an unpowered site for a night. Was made to wait whilst the receptionist finished a phone call but noticed that they had an online booking system powered by AI. I prefer the personal touch so I waited for the phone call to end. I was given a site number & shown how to get to it on a map. Upon arriving at said site, I noticed that it backed into what appeared to be a permanent residents abode. I went to find the toilets & discovered that they were a considerable distance away. The whole arrangement was totally wrong for me so I decided to get a refund & try elsewhere. I explained my dilemma to the young fellow who then offered me the choice of 2 sites. The one I chose was going to be ideal. It had a concrete slab upon which I could  park my vehicle which had a Rooftop tent. The toilets were right outside so I accepted this site instead of getting a refund & moving on. Big mistake! As I was leaving the office it was made known to me that " I won't charge you the extra $10 for power."  How generous considering that I had just paid $40 to park on some sand with the added prospect of being watched by someone in the hovel behind me. As darkness fell I showered & climbed up the ladder to go to bed. Not long after a vehicle pulled up. I thought that it sounded close but paid no attention until  a sliding door was being opened & closed repeatedly. Somewhere close by other voices seemed to be having a little soiree. This went on until way after 9pm. Imagine my shock when, upon getting up to go to the toilets I noticed a huge campervan parked no more than a metre from where I was sleeping. If I opened my flyscreen window I probably could have kissed the occupants of the campervan goodnight!! Now this caravan park is very big on rules apparently. Perusing their website sets out the terms & conditions upon which you, as a guest, must adhere & they take great pains to advise you of what they shall charge you if you have the temerity to break said rules! Ve haf vays off making you pay! Ya? It's more like Stalag Luft 13 than a caravan park! As it is, I am writing this at 2.20am because I am so angry that the site next to me contains, a caravan, 2 vehicles & a huge campervan. The website clearly states how many vehicles, can be accommodated on each site yet next door looks like a used car lot! Will I ever come back? You must be joking!!
This caravan park is very quiet and very clean.  A little old but I found it very nice and also great prices. You will need a car to get you to the beach and shops.

We stayed for one night at a powered site and were very pleased. Nice cooking facilities and some of the cleanest public wash room facilities I've ever seen. Clean pool and pet friendly is always a nice touch.
Staff were nice and very accommodating after a long drive.  Only 10 minutes from everything but far enough away from any main roads that you hear no traffic at all. Will be back

The caravan park is a little way out of town, but that is a good thing. It was quiet, which is a lovely change for us as we have been to a lot of the bigger named parks where kids & dogs were given free rein!!
And we had a grass mat!!!! We have only had hard dirt for weeks. It was luxury.
Yes it’s dated, but the amenities were very clean & fresh.
I recommend a stay here if you love peace & quiet.
Lovely and quiet, clean amenities, staff very friendly and helpful.

Stayed here overnight on a powered site on the way up the coast.
Clean facilities, good camp kitchen, everything I needed.

Excellent park with great facilities.
Friendly staff with everything you need in one place.
Hot water in shower, plumbing worked. Basic amenities in old cabin. Steps .

Excellent place with good facilities, the bathrooms are clean and have showers with hot water, the spaces for camping are really good.
The staff is the best part, very friendly and willing to help you with whatever you need.
We will definitely come back. <3

Nice office girl but that's about it!  The site was sandy & no green grass around. Pool was closed & showers were old & mouldy. For $52 a night for a pw site we expected alot better than that. Will not be back
Stayed there over a year ago for 10 days, best decision for your first stop in Gero. Thanks guys
We love to come back here with our friends
Great location and very competitively priced for an overnight stay on the way to Ningaloo. Staff were super helpful after I stuffed up my booking. Thank you! We were well looked after. Great service.

This place is a dump..dirt rds full of pot holes..grounds unkept..overgrown gardend dead and full of weeds..an advertised pool that has been closed down by the council due to it's condition..dirty bbq area. Permanents areas unkept..rude staff and at $44 per night for a powered site was sooo much overpriced..don't come here..it's not worth it

We stayed for one night at Geraldton Caravan Park on a non-powered site. The ground was sandy, and although our neighbors were a bit noisy during the day (with some barking dogs), it was quiet at night. The park itself felt a bit dated but still functional.

The bathroom facilities were spacious, with plenty of toilets and showers, and they were generally clean. A fun bonus was the radio playing loud in the bathroom—such a cool touch! Overall, a decent stop for a night, though the park could use a bit of an update.

The Pool is Closed for maintenance and I was lied to when I booked.||They refuse to take down the photographs I was offered my money back after we just spent an hour in the heat setting up or a shower.||The money these thieves expect us to pay for a spare block of land is criminal.

We checked in after hours 10.30pm and made prior arrangements for this.  Once we got there and made our way to the cottage, we were greeted upon entry through the front door a very musky, rodent like smell and then saw a big roach scattering along the kitchen floor.  The walls to the cottage are very thin, the care taker/manager came to us and warned of excessive noise as we were trying to unpack the vehicles, we were just excited to finally get there, and gave us a warning of the noise curfew.  I bought a can of Glenn10 to deodorise the cottage due to the smell being old and musky, leaking taps in the bathroom also.  To top it of the booklet for visitors to read for all the tc's was pages of how much you will pay if you don't leave the premises as you found it.  2k for the lounge suite, 1k for the tv, $35 to replace towels, $5 for toilet paper, cleaning costs, the list went on.  I decided not to even cook, bought our own toilet paper and towels from the Gero town ship and decided to just eat out to avoid being penalised to use kitchen if we left it to a degree that was unexceptable to the park facilitators and charged.
Phone and email service was awesome, but couldn't settle into the cottage as I just continued telling my family to keep the noise down and not to touch anything.
Never experienced a weekend getaway like this, a very uncomfortable experience for our family
WORST PARK DONT GO HERE MEAN STAFF WEIRD PEOPLE AND BAD FACILITIES
Not good you have permanent living mixed with holiday makers. Advertise a pool but you can't use it would not recommend to any family 😕
Great cabin for overnight stay with dog. Clean amenities, great service and well organised park ground.

Worst place I've stayed smoke buts all over caravan park park is porley maintained plus management are disrespectfull to customers because accommodation is hard to find way to expensive  for wat thay offer  thay where rude to My children aswell

不錯的Caravan Park
有自助check in 和booking 機台
很方便
廁所浴室很乾淨
有時候會有蟑螂跑出來
廁所乾淨 明亮 還有放音樂，廚房小巧可愛，推薦，櫃檯有自助式入住非常方便
AMAZING stay, very quiet, and felt so so safe. Pet friendly, and incredibly friendly and helpful staff, Thank you for an excellent 2 nights Dean! We will be back!

This is a reasonable caravan park,  competitively priced for the area. However I don't understand why they need an expensive flashy automatic check in kiosk. All your personal details are on display and you have to provide details like your email address even if you don't want to. It charged us an extra dollar than the website price,  presumably to pay for the thing. It crashed on the last page and we had to get a human to assign us a site. 10 minutes after we set up, the kiosk sent us a different site number. The money spent on this unnecessary technology would be better spent on upgrading the bathrooms.

Felt like isolated from the world.
Really good experience with a calm and silent moment.
There are some people with long-stay and they were kind to talk.
The internet conditions were not so fast.

What a great little caravan park, A little old school just how I like it, And a good friendly mix of residents with super friendly staff,  clean ablutions … Don’t stop accommodating all, It’s tough out there at the moment for some.
Tutto orribile. Attenzione ai ratti. Cucina e bagni incrostati. Siamo scappate e non ci hanno nemmeno dato il rimborso… BOOO

The reception staff are friendly but that's where the good stuff ends.  |The park is old, tired and very run down with potholed roads.  The web site is impressive but that's as good as it gets and beware there is one price for online booking and another for in person.|Cheap price doesn't equal good value, I seriously recommend you look elsewhere.  |I WON'T BE BACK

Don't bother stopping here, the management staff are friendly enough but that's where the good stuff ends.  There are more permanent sites than guests, the facilities are tired and grubby, the sites are unkempt and the roads potholed, I could go on but I won't.  KEEP MOVING.
Camped on an unpowered lot. Had lots of space and a beautiful tree for shade. Also nice and quiet.
Quiet caravan park. Friendly staff at reception. Very neglected, ablutions mopped but not cleaned, dump point available but blocked or full.
Good quiet park, a little out of town but well worth it for the quiet.
Redelijke camping met wifi, grote plaatsen, camperskitchen watertappunten. Niet duur

Es gibt viel Klos und heise Duschen.
Die Seifenspender an den Waschbecken waren auf der Damen & Herren Toilette leer. Die duschen sind alt und sehr niedlich (nicht für Leute über 160cm geeignet).
Für den Power side Anschluss mussten wir über den Stellplatz unseres Nachbars das Kabel verlegen, da die Anschlussbox so weit entfernt war.
Ab 7 Uhr wurden die Bäume gestutzt…

This caravan park has automated check in, which doesn't let you check in until after 2.00pm and doesn't give you a map, just a site number. If you need help to check in you have to ring a number provided. The amenities are average, but clean. They don't have a bbq. This park is 10km out of Geraldton on a back road.

Stayed for 1 night at a powered site with our caravan in mid August. Pretty decent overall. Was crowded but they managed to squeeze 2 lots for my friends and I as we booked last minute on the day itself.

Communal kitchen available. Plenty of toilets and hot water showers that are decently clean, but I foresee the cleanliness won’t be too good in the summer.

We booked in with our caravan on a drive through sit. It was not drive through at all. Camp kitchen does not have an oven. No TV service.  Very old amenities. Surprisingly it was booked out. Next time I'll stay somewhere else.

Simple park that could be beautiful if the manager cared enough to inspect the work of her cleaners because the bathrooms are terrible cleaned between 9-11 and yet still smells like urine at 11.01? Non smoking bathroom but permanent residents allowed to smoke inside toilets with management knowledge also children are not very welcome here
Good value stay, would certainly stay again.
Absolutely love our 5 day stay! Very well maintained and beautiful people running it too! Will definitely stay again

Super lovely staff and very accommodating. I originally booked for a tent site but changed to a cabin last minute as it was raining and the process was made so easy! I stayed over my birthday weekend and the staff even put up a birthday banner which was very sweet! The dog area for dogs to run around off lead was also super handy, and the toilets and showers were kept very clean! The cabins got quite warm overnight even in the winter months so I'd recommend keeping the windows cracked. Kitchen in the cabin was stocked with all your basic cooking utensils, pans, etc.
Average
Great park friendly staff clean amenities we had a great week thanks guys.

Lovely lady at the front counter willing to help in any way, very very quiet at night, been here 12 nights car being fixed by SIVS AUTO FULLY RECOMMEND MICHAEL AND CLAIRE.CLEAN amenities.bit far to town with no car , and to far to walk to bus 5klms would stay again

April 2024
Decided to have a long trip from Denham to Perth broken up by a stopover in Geraldton overnight. We managed to book a park cabin at very short notice, plus we were able to park our caravan in a nearby van site overnight. Service was fantastic- very friendly and very helpful.
Many permanent resident sites.
The pool was neat and tidy with bbq area / camp kitchen under a half cover. Was inviting. Ablutions were neat and tidy- hot showers and decent toilets. The park cabin itself was a little disappointing. Yes there was an air con but it was in the main bedroom. It needed to be on high to unsuccessfully attempt to cool the small place down. The bigger area where the kids slept didn’t get any cool at all. Thankfully there was a pedestal fan the kids could use.
Early evening it rained lightly. As we rested on our bed main bedroom the ceiling leaked directly on us, so we had to swivel the bed around to not get wet. Thankfully it wasn’t a long downpour! The PowerPoint in the main bedroom nearly came off the wall when unplugging plugs.
With some money spent on it to fix some minor things, this park could be better than what it is. It’s - ok. Service- fantastic.

Getting a cabin felt like staying in a air bnb. I had quite the bugger around, being told I needed to change rooms after the first night. For unknown reason. Then charged an extra surcharge for the second room for the second nights stay, then told after i had finished cleaning the room after first night's stay, that I no longar had to change room.  aswell as sings up every where telling u to clean fridge/microwave/oven and even empty the bin!! Before leaving otherwise to be charged an extra $38 (why pay $170 a night to be told to leave cabin spotless. (What a joke.) Washing mechines are a charge of $6 a laod. Instead of 3or4 not to mention that I was changed an extra $10 a night to have my dog stay out side the room.
Very convenient location
May be an uplifting can enhance the potential of the place. However, hats off to the hoist, Lisa was wonderful.
A bit outdated but very clean. The staff were super helpful..
Good place for the price, very nice staff and clean amenities

Love staying here, have stayed 4 years in a row. The pool is great to cool off after a long hot day and the rooms are dog friendly which is super rare these days !! The current park managers are super friendly and very helpful. Just make sure you bring bottled water as Geraldton water leaves a lot to be desired 😂
Really nice layed back park with spotless amenities, very helpful staff & great atmosphere. Away from the hustle and bustle & just feels good.

We were extremely inconvenienced by this place. They booked us in. Took our money. We left home with our three dogs and drove for over an hour from Shark Bay toward Geraldton and staff first messaged us to let us know they'd made a terrible mistake and double booked us. We had no other accommodation options as we are travelling with dogs last minute. It was only after we didn't answer the message that they had the courtesy to call and let us know. We've been forced to stay in a hotel and the dogs have to sleep in the car. So very disappointing.

The hosts were excellent. The park itself could do with a good tidy up. We had the cottage which was very basic and spent time cleaning and the shower had hair from a previous occupant. The cutlery, mugs etc left a lot to be disired. There was a notice telling guest to leave as we find , hmmm! And how the they could charge $30 for extra cleaning is a joke, do it well for you paying guests to start with!!
We stayed here in our caravan. Peaceful and quiet - even during school holidays! Would highly recommend. Lovely owners! Would stay here again . It’s very affordable for travelling families. :)
Nice park a bit out of town but quite and clean.
No TV reception is available

The lady and gentlemen  I interacted with were both lovely...very helpful and obliging.
This was a quick overnight trip for a family wedding and the accommodation  was just what the 5 of us needed.
Very clean cottage and close to the shower facilities.
Would not hesitate  to stay here again and the price was very reasonable.
Great place to stay and pet friendly which helps when travelling

Don’t be fooled by the word Chalet. I had a different picture to what was rented to us. Lots of broken floor titles, moulding and framing deteriorating or not there, spoil the aesthetics.  Bedroom fan didn’t work. No bedside lights. No soap or hair wash products. Comfortable bed though. Needs major refurbishment.

Such a nice caravan park, we spent two nights there in an unpowered site (40$/night)
Very clean toilet/shower, a nice pool and a clean kitchen.
Owners are so nice, they brought us wood and a tin letting us to have a fire.
Next time I’ll know where to stop in Geraldton for sure!

The receptionist really makes this place amazing! It is very simple with simple ammenities but that is reflecting in a super cheap price. The staff bend over backwards for you! Really great place! With super friendly staff it also seems to rub off on the caravaners because they were also super friendly and helpful too!
We were towed here as car broken down. We stayed in our caravan. Nice & quiet. Excellent park for that reason.
Very friendly service and enjoyed my week stay , toilets and showers 🚿 very clean and grounds well kept and dog and kid friendly  , enjoyed my time there
Rustic park, very quiet and close to town.
Very quiet peaceful camping grounds. Management staff are very friendly. Bathrooms are very clean. Good area out the back to let your dog go for a run.

Wonderful hosts Steve and Lisa. A great location 10 minutes out of town with a park for dogs to be walked at the end of the park. Camp kitchen and laundry and bathrooms  cleaned daily. Very reasonable  price even though  it was school holidays

Excellent caravan park, good camp kitchen and ablution facilities good friendly service.
A few Kilometers out of Geraldton but it is in the right direction going North, recommended
Needs better WiFi, get Starlink
Staying in camper bus on site with a friend. He is a permanent guest here. I like it as it's away from the bustle of town and is very quiet. Very casual atmosphere. Perfect for sunset drinks.
